insufficientIds: Wayne Coyne (amp not corroborated by 2+ whitelisted sources)
_meta.note: Densification vague 2 (bassistes manquants + groupes phares + retries insufficient). Exclut les 244 ids/names déjà en base (seed + batch v1). Merge dans artists.js à valider.

marcus_miller.name: Marcus Miller

guitars: Fender Marcus Miller Signature Jazz Bass

marcus_miller.notes: His Sadowsky-modified 1977 Fender Jazz Bass with active preamp defined the slap-driven fusion/R&B bass tone; later got Fender and SWR signature gear.

victor_wooten.name: Victor Wooten
victor_wooten.notes: Virtuoso of slap, double-thumping and tapping; his Fodera basses (notably the 1983 Monarch 'number 1' and Yin Yang) through Hartke amps are his signature setup with the Flecktones.

bernard_edwards.name: Bernard Edwards
bernard_edwards.notes: Architect of the disco/funk groove; his Music Man StingRay (the 'Good Times bass') and clean fingerstyle defined Chic's sound, mostly recorded via DI.

louis_johnson.name: Louis Johnson
louis_johnson.notes: 'Thunder-Thumbs' slap pioneer who helped design the Music Man StingRay; played on Michael Jackson's Off the Wall and Thriller ('Billie Jean', 'Don't Stop 'Til You Get Enough').

john_wetton.name: John Wetton
john_wetton.notes: Bassist and lead vocalist; his growling '61 Precision through Hiwatt tube amps with natural overdrive defined the heavy King Crimson era before fronting Asia.

mike_rutherford.name: Mike Rutherford
mike_rutherford.notes: Plays bass and guitar in Genesis; early Rickenbacker/Shergold basses and a custom Rickenbacker double-neck, with Moog Taurus pedals adding low end during his guitar passages.

lemmy_kilmister.name: Lemmy Kilmister
lemmy_kilmister.notes: Bassist and lead vocalist of Motorhead; played a Rickenbacker on the treble pickup with everything maxed straight into hot-rodded Marshall Super Bass heads, his bass roaring like a distorted guitar.

phil_lesh.name: Phil Lesh
phil_lesh.notes: Melodic, counterpoint-driven Grateful Dead bassist; pioneered Alembic active basses and quadraphonic per-string outputs feeding the band's legendary Wall of Sound, powered by McIntosh amps.

john_mcvie.name: John McVie
john_mcvie.notes: Solid, supportive Fleetwood Mac bassist; mostly Fender Precision/Jazz basses, with a 1976 Alembic famously used on 'The Chain' from Rumours; switched to Orange amps in later years.

tim_bogert.name: Tim Bogert
tim_bogert.notes: Pioneering hard-rock bass virtuoso known for fast runs, slides and fuzzed-up tones; played a hybrid Precision body with a Telecaster Bass neck through Sunn and Acoustic rigs.

aston_barrett.name: Aston "Family Man" Barrett
aston_barrett.notes: Bassist, bandleader and co-producer of Bob Marley & The Wailers; his Fender Jazz Bass picked near the neck joint through an Acoustic 360 defined the deep, rounded reggae bottom end.

robbie_shakespeare.name: Robbie Shakespeare
robbie_shakespeare.notes: Half of the prolific Sly & Robbie rhythm section; his Fender Jazz Bass and later PRS/Markbass rig shaped reggae and dub bass, usually going direct to the board live.

jeff_berlin.name: Jeff Berlin
jeff_berlin.notes: Fusion virtuoso who rose with drummer Bill Bruford's band; played heavily modified Fender Precision/Jazz basses (Bartolini pickups, Badass bridge, custom electronics), later with Dean and Markbass signature gear.

stuart_hamm.name: Stuart Hamm
stuart_hamm.notes: Two-handed tapping and slapping virtuoso known for his work with Joe Satriani and Steve Vai; played the Kubicki X Factor in his Satriani years and was the first bassist to get a Fender signature model (the Urge), through Hartke rigs.

chris_hillman.name: Chris Hillman
chris_hillman.notes: Mandolinist-turned-bassist who joined The Byrds on bass; his Guild Starfire with flatwounds (then a Fender Precision live) underpinned 'Turn, Turn, Turn!' and 'Eight Miles High'.

mike_watt.name: Mike Watt
mike_watt.notes: Aggressive, melodic punk/jazz-punk bassist; switched from Gibson EB-3 to a Fender Telecaster Bass in the mid-Minutemen era and to Gallien-Krueger amps for fIREHOSE.

mick_karn.name: Mick Karn
mick_karn.notes: Pioneering fretless bassist with an instantly recognizable rubbery, melodic style; played an aluminium-neck Travis Bean through Gentlemen Take Polaroids, then switched to Wal basses for Tin Drum and his solo work.

klaus_flouride.name: Klaus Flouride
klaus_flouride.notes: Hardcore punk pioneer; played a 1966 Lake Placid Blue Fender Jazz Bass through an Acoustic 150B (later a Traynor) for the Dead Kennedys' run, with a Fender Bass VI on Bedtime for Democracy.

andy_rourke.name: Andy Rourke
andy_rourke.notes: Melodic, funk-influenced bassist of The Smiths; played a 1964 sunburst Fender Precision Bass (plus a Yamaha BB2000 for funkier lines) through Peavey, then Trace Elliot and Ampeg rigs.

simon_gallup.name: Simon Gallup
simon_gallup.notes: Driving, chorus-and-flanger-soaked bass anchor of The Cure; long associated with Fender Jazz Basses through Ampeg SVT stacks before moving to Schecter signature models in the 2010s.

steve_digiorgio.name: Steve Di Giorgio
steve_digiorgio.notes: Credited as the first bassist to bring fretless playing into extreme metal; played custom fretless basses with Sadus, Death and Control Denied through Ampeg/Randall, later moving to Ibanez and EBS rigs.

frank_bello.name: Frank Bello

guitars: Fender Frank Bello Aerodyne Jazz Bass

frank_bello.notes: Energetic, pick-driven thrash bassist of Anthrax; long associated with Fender Jazz Basses through Ampeg SVT stacks with a SansAmp for grind, later switching to a Hartke rig.

david_ellefson.name: David Ellefson
david_ellefson.notes: Foundational thrash bassist of Megadeth; used B.C. Rich Mockingbird basses (the fretless Eagle birthed the Peace Sells line) in the 80s before settling into a long-running Jackson signature and Hartke rig.

robert_trujillo.name: Robert Trujillo
robert_trujillo.notes: Aggressive slap-and-fingerstyle bassist; during his Suicidal Tendencies and Infectious Grooves years he played Tobias and Fernandes basses through Ampeg SVT-2 Pro stacks.

tom_araya.name: Tom Araya
tom_araya.notes: Bassist and lead vocalist of Slayer; played a twin-pickup B.C. Rich Wave through the thrash years before adopting an ESP signature and stock Marshall VBA400 heads with a dbx compressor.

joey_demaio.name: Joey DeMaio
joey_demaio.notes: Bombastic power-metal bassist of Manowar known for piccolo-tuned bass and wall-of-amps theatrics; plays custom EMG-loaded Rickenbacker 4003 and Dawk Demon basses through heavily modified rigs.

krist_novoselic.name: Krist Novoselic
krist_novoselic.notes: Grunge cornerstone bassist of Nirvana; the Gibson Ripper was his signature live bass through an Ampeg SVT-400T driving Mesa Boogie cabs, later a heavily modified Hiwatt DR103.

jeff_ament.name: Jeff Ament
jeff_ament.notes: Inventive grunge bassist of Pearl Jam; pioneered multi-string Hamer basses (the 12-string famously on 'Jeremy') through vintage Ampeg SVT rigs.

ben_shepherd.name: Ben Shepherd
ben_shepherd.notes: Heavy, downtuned grunge bassist of Soundgarden; recorded Badmotorfinger and Superunknown on a '72 Fender Jazz Bass through a Mesa Boogie 400B, later running a split Ampeg/Mesa Carbine rig.

mike_inez.name: Mike Inez
mike_inez.notes: Hard-picking metal/grunge bassist who played with Ozzy Osbourne before joining Alice in Chains; favors Warwick Streamer and EMG-loaded Spector basses through stacks of Ampeg SVTs.

darcy_wretzky.name: D'arcy Wretzky
darcy_wretzky.notes: Foundational bassist of The Smashing Pumpkins; her sticker-covered 'Rat Bass' Fender Precision and Jazz Basses cut through the wall-of-guitars via Trace Elliot, later Mesa Boogie, amplification.

nate_mendel.name: Nate Mendel
nate_mendel.notes: Punchy, pick-and-finger bassist for Sunny Day Real Estate and Foo Fighters; played a 1971 Fender Precision for almost his entire career (basis for his signature model), moving to an Ashdown rig in later years.

matt_freeman.name: Matt Freeman
matt_freeman.notes: Lightning-fingered punk bassist of Operation Ivy and Rancid; built his sound on a 1977 Fender Precision and vintage Fender Jazz Basses through Ampeg, later Fender Bassman, amplification.

robby_krieger.name: Robby Krieger

guitars: Gibson SG RK-1 Robby Krieger Signature

robby_krieger.notes: Doors guitarist whose fingerstyle Gibson SG into rented Fender Twin Reverbs defined the band's studio tone (Light My Fire). His original 1964 SG Special was stolen.

don_felder.name: Don Felder
don_felder.notes: Co-author of Hotel California; recorded its solos on a 1959 Les Paul through a '50s Tweed Deluxe, and modified an Olympic White Gibson EDS-1275 doubleneck to dual-output it for the live performance.

carl_wilson.name: Carl Wilson
carl_wilson.notes: Lead guitarist of the Beach Boys whose Olympic White Fender Jaguar into Fender Dual Showman amps shaped the early surf-rock guitar sound.

brian_wilson.name: Brian Wilson
brian_wilson.notes: Beach Boys founder, primary songwriter and original bassist; played a white Fender Precision Bass through a Fender Bassman / Ampeg B-15 before retiring from live touring in 1965.

roger_fisher.name: Roger Fisher
roger_fisher.notes: Original Heart guitarist; used a 1965 Les Paul Gold Top into a Fender Twin Reverb for Dreamboat Annie, and a Stratocaster through a Music Man head for the Barracuda riff.

rick_derringer.name: Rick Derringer
rick_derringer.notes: Author/guitarist of Rock and Roll, Hoochie Koo; played a Gibson SG Standard with Edgar Winter through Ampeg/Marshall stacks in the '70s before moving to Strats and signature Warrior guitars.

trey_azagthoth.name: Trey Azagthoth
trey_azagthoth.notes: Morbid Angel's founding guitarist; signature death-metal tone built on B.C. Rich Ironbird guitars into Marshall JCM900 dual-reverb heads with a ProCo Rat and MXR modulation.

hank_shermann.name: Hank Shermann
hank_shermann.notes: Founding Mercyful Fate guitarist; recorded the early albums on a DiMarzio-loaded Strat through 100-watt Marshall Super Lead and JCM800 heads.
